items.description: A testable unit of system functionality with a defined contract

fields.description: The behavioral contract this behavior guarantees
fields.name: contract

fields.description: Invariants this behavior enforces
fields.name: invariants

fields.description: Type definitions used by this behavior
fields.name: types

fields.description: Port interfaces this behavior interacts with
fields.name: ports

fields.description: Events produced as a result of this behavior
fields.name: produces

fields.description: Events this behavior reacts to
fields.name: consumes

fields.description: Classification tag for agent task routing
fields.name: category

fields.description: Product features this behavior implements
fields.name: features

fields.description: Human-readable summary of this behavior
fields.name: description

fields.description: Current lifecycle status of this behavior
fields.name: status

fields.description: External references such as issue or document URIs
fields.name: refs

fields.description: Impact level if this behavior fails
fields.name: severity

fields.description: Diagnostic message for validation tooling
fields.name: diagnostic

items.inference_guide: Look for public functions in service, handler, or controller layers that represent user-visible operations or business logic. Route handlers (HTTP, gRPC, CLI commands), use-case functions, and domain service methods are strong signals. Map function name to entity ID (snake_case). Extract doc comments or the function's purpose as the contract. If the function validates rules or enforces constraints, those are invariants. If it emits events or messages, those are produces. If it has dedicated test files or test functions, add verify statements. Skip: private helpers, utility functions, test fixtures, generated code, framework boilerplate.
items.name: Behavior

items.description: A system-wide constraint that must always hold true

fields.description: The constraint this invariant guarantees holds at all times
fields.name: guarantee

fields.description: Consequence or impact if this invariant is violated
fields.name: risk

fields.description: Human-readable summary of this invariant
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references such as issue or document URIs
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for assertions, validation logic, and defensive checks that enforce system-wide rules. Signals: assert!() / assert_eq!() statements, guard clauses that panic or return errors, database constraints (UNIQUE, CHECK, NOT NULL), middleware that rejects invalid state, config validation at startup, and comments like 'must always', 'never allow', 'invariant'. The guarantee field should state what must hold (e.g., 'User email must be unique across all accounts'). The risk field describes consequences of violation. Invariants are architectural rules about state; for quantified limits (latency <200ms, max connections), use constraint instead. Skip: local variable checks, input validation that's behavior-specific, temporary debug assertions.
items.name: Invariant

items.description: A significant occurrence in the system that triggers reactions

fields.description: Communication channel this event is published on
fields.name: channel

fields.description: Transport mechanism for the channel (e.g. queue, topic, stream)
fields.name: channel_type

fields.description: Classification of this event (e.g. domain, integration, system)
fields.name: category

fields.description: Type definition describing this event's data shape
fields.name: payload

fields.description: Human-readable summary of this event
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references such as issue or document URIs
fields.name: refs

fields.description: Delivery or ordering guarantees for this event
fields.name: contract

items.inference_guide: Look for message types, event classes, pub/sub topics, webhook payloads, and signal/notification patterns. Signals: structs/classes named *Event, *Message, *Notification; message queue topic/channel definitions; emit()/publish()/dispatch()/notify() calls; event handler registrations (on_*, handle_*); webhook payload schemas. Map the event name to entity ID. If it carries structured data, reference the payload type. Identify which behaviors produce and consume each event. Skip: internal method calls, logging statements, framework lifecycle callbacks (unless domain-meaningful).
items.name: Event

items.description: A data structure or domain model definition

fields.description: The type category (e.g. struct, enum, alias, opaque)
fields.name: kind

fields.description: Structured field definitions for this type
fields.name: fields

fields.description: Types composed or referenced by this type
fields.name: composed_types

fields.description: Parent type this type extends or inherits from
fields.name: extends

fields.description: Human-readable summary of this type
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references such as issue or document URIs
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for domain model structs, data transfer objects, API request/response shapes, database entities, and enum definitions that carry business meaning. Signals: struct/class definitions in models/ or domain/ directories; TypeScript interfaces/types for API contracts; protobuf/GraphQL/JSON Schema type definitions; ORM model classes; enum types with business variants. Use open_fields to list the type's fields. Set kind field to 'struct', 'enum', 'alias', or 'opaque'. Reference composed_types for nested or referenced types. Skip: internal implementation structs, builder patterns, framework-generated types, test fixtures.
items.name: Type

items.description: An interface boundary between system components

fields.description: Whether this port is inbound, outbound, or bidirectional
fields.name: direction

fields.description: Classification of this port (e.g. http, grpc, database, queue)
fields.name: category

fields.description: Method signatures exposed by this port interface
fields.name: methods

fields.description: Human-readable summary of this port
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references such as issue or document URIs
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for trait definitions, abstract classes, interface declarations, and adapter patterns that define boundaries between system layers. Signals: Rust traits in ports/ or interfaces/ directories; TypeScript/Java interfaces for repositories, gateways, or external services; abstract base classes; dependency injection interfaces; API client contracts. Set direction to 'inbound' (receives requests), 'outbound' (calls external systems), or 'bidirectional'. Use open_fields/methods to list the port's operations. Set category to 'http', 'grpc', 'database', 'queue', 'filesystem', etc. Skip: internal module boundaries, utility traits (Display, Debug), marker traits, framework-imposed interfaces.
items.name: Port
